methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Nord Quantique is a seed-stage quantum computing company that has raised C$40M+ in total funding, including a C$23M investment from Quantum Champions in December 2025. The company operates within a sector defined by 3 public comparables (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers, including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to Nord Quantique. The quantum computing sector contains only 7 same-sector peers overall, but filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier companies yields fewer than three datapoints in any single cohort—below the threshold required for reliable median-based triangulation. Attempting to apply an all-sector median would introduce material bias and obscure the distinct capital structures and development trajectories within quantum hardware.

The absence of sufficient narrow comparables reflects the nascent nature of the quantum computing hardware market, where stage heterogeneity and funding dynamics vary substantially across the competitive set. A defensible valuation framework for Nord Quantique would require alternative methods—such as venture capital method analysis keyed to funding stage and capital efficiency, or structured scenario modeling around technical milestones and market adoption timelines. Without quantitative peer data meeting minimum thresholds, any single-method valuation carry unquantified assumption risk.
